sources_audited.note: The exact Census profile URL resolves, but the reopened page exposes only a JavaScript-required shell in the validator's text retriever, so the 212-establishment figure and definition could not be directly inspected.

The BLS page is explicitly the May 2023 national occupation-and-wage table for broader NAICS 334100 and shows the cited management, computer, engineering, sales, office, production, maintenance, and logistics occupation groups.

NIST documents the cited manufacturing AI use cases in maintenance, quality, forecasting, inventory and resource management, scheduling, supply chains, inspection, and digital twins, together with data, cost, skills, cybersecurity, and legacy-integration barriers.

Dell's fiscal 2025 Form 10-K supports strong AI-optimized server demand and growth, weaker consumer-client performance, competitive pricing pressure, component and vendor economics, support revenue, and lower gross-margin percentages associated with AI-server mix and client pricing.

The exact Census AIES table URL resolves, but the reopened page exposes only a JavaScript-required shell in the validator's text retriever, so the stated 2023 sales-or-shipments and operating-expense values could not be directly inspected.
BLS Table 2.11 lists broader NAICS 334100 real output of $43.5 billion in 2024 and $51.0 billion in 2034, with a 1.6% compound annual growth rate.

The SBA blog reports that 47% of owners expecting to retire within five years lack a successor; the record accurately treats this older, cross-industry, secondary statistic only as a broad succession proxy.

caveats: S1's exact Census profile was reachable only as a JavaScript shell in the validator's text retriever, so its definition and 212-establishment statistic were not directly inspectable; this is not material because e is already an ESTIMATE, o is judgmental, and the frozen firm-count input rather than this establishment count drives the base availability calculation.

S5's exact Census AIES table was reachable only as a JavaScript shell in the validator's text retriever, so the stated sales-or-shipments and operating-expense figures were not directly inspectable; those figures are contextual rather than the numeric basis of the e estimate and do not change the base tier or action.

The a primitive combines a broader 334100 occupational mix with generic manufacturing AI use cases rather than measuring wage-weighted remaining exposure for 334111 LMM firms. The record labels this PROXY, states the bridge and error directions, and provides a wide range.

The e primitive translates establishments and a frozen margin-bridged firm count into eligible transferable firms without a named-candidate review. It is appropriately labeled ESTIMATE and is already the base score's weakest-link factor.

The q and d5 bridges extrapolate from Dell, whose scale, purchasing power, channels, and AI-server mix differ materially from LMM specialist computer builders. The mismatch is disclosed, the ranges are broad, and correcting the broad demand growth proxy alone would not change the capped base D factor or the model-conditioned action.

The s5 bridge rests on a 2018 SBA blog repeating a cross-industry SCORE succession statistic, not observed completed control transfers in 334111. The record explicitly labels it PROXY and discounts it, so this weakness does not warrant rejection.
summary: Mechanics pass. The record is a useful and internally coherent estimate-heavy screen: evidence states are honest, broad-source bridges and their error directions are disclosed, ranges cover meaningful uncertainty, the default lens is coherent, and the narrative follows the registered evidence. Two Census statistics could not be directly inspected because the exact cited pages require JavaScript, while the remaining five primitive-cited sources support their stated uses. No source or construct defect demonstrated a correction that would change the CONDITIONAL base tier or VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action, so the record is publishable with caveats.
